title: Sikkal Singaravelan Temple
tagline: Sikkal Singaravelan Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Sikkal Singara Velar Temple is one of the most popular Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Muruga and a contender for the not unofficial seventh Padaiveedu of Muruga, along with the po
overview.description: Sikkal Singara Velar Temple is one of the most popular Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Muruga and a contender for the not unofficial seventh Padaiveedu of Muruga, along with the popular Arupadaiveedu (six abodes of Lord Muruga). It is one of the rare traditional Hindu temples that has both Shiva and Vishnu deities in the same complex.

topPlaces.name: Sikkal
topPlaces.description: Sikkal is a small township located between Thiruvarur and Nagapattinam in Tamil Nadu, India. It is 6 km west of Nagapattinam, 18 km east of Thiruvarur.

topPlaces.name: Puliyur, Nagapattinam
topPlaces.description: Puliyur is a village in Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is situated 7 km from Nagapattinam, District Headquarters and 3 km from Sikkal.

topPlaces.name: Nagapattinam taluk
topPlaces.description: Nagapattinam taluk is a taluk of Nagapattinam district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Nagapattinam block
topPlaces.description: Nagapattinam block is a revenue block in the Nagapattinam taluk of Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. There are a total of 27 panchayat villages in this block.

topPlaces.name: Nagapattinam division
topPlaces.description: Nagapattinam division is a revenue division of Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu, India. It comprises the taluks of Kilvelur, Nagapattinam, Thirukkuvalai and Vedaranyam.

topPlaces.name: Loganatha Perumal Temple
topPlaces.description: The Loganatha Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located 2 km away from Sikkal, Tamil Nadu, India on the Tiruvarur-Nagapatnam highway. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshiped as Loganatha Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Loganayagi.

topPlaces.name: Action of 6 July 1746
topPlaces.description: The action of 6 July 1746 was an inconclusive naval engagement between the British and French fleets during the War of the Austrian Succession.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Negapatam (1758)
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Nagapatam was an indecisive naval battle on 3 August 1758 during the Seven Years' War. A British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock fought a French squadron under Comte d'Aché off the Carnatic coast of India near Negapatam in the second of three battles fought between the two admirals during the war.

items.note: Sikkal is a small township located between Thiruvarur and Nagapattinam in Tamil Nadu, India. It is 6 km west of Nagapattinam, 18 km east of Thiruvarur.
Puliyur is a village in Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is situated 7 km from Nagapattinam, District Headquarters and 3 km from Sikkal.
Nagapattinam taluk is a taluk of Nagapattinam district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
Nagapattinam block is a revenue block in the Nagapattinam taluk of Nagapattinam district, Tamil Nadu, India. There are a total of 27 panchayat villages in this block.
Nagapattinam division is a revenue division of Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu, India. It comprises the taluks of Kilvelur, Nagapattinam, Thirukkuvalai and Vedaranyam.

The Loganatha Perumal Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu located 2 km away from Sikkal, Tamil Nadu, India on the Tiruvarur-Nagapatnam highway. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshiped as Loganatha Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Loganayagi.
The action of 6 July 1746 was an inconclusive naval engagement between the British and French fleets during the War of the Austrian Succession.

The Battle of Nagapatam was an indecisive naval battle on 3 August 1758 during the Seven Years' War. A British squadron under Vice-Admiral George Pocock fought a French squadron under Comte d'Aché off the Carnatic coast of India near Negapatam in the second of three battles fought between the two admirals during the war.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sikkal Singaravelan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sikkal Singaravelan Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sikkal Singaravelan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (119 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (121 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sikkal Singaravelan Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sikkal Singaravelan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sikkal Singaravelan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sikkal Singaravelan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sikkal Singaravelan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sikkal, Puliyur, Nagapattinam, Nagapattinam taluk, Nagapattinam block, Nagapattinam division.
